skill.name: ml-engineer
skill.description: Build production ML systems with PyTorch 2.x, TensorFlow, and modern ML frameworks. Implements model serving, feature engineering, A/B testing, and monitoring. Use PROACTIVELY for ML model deployment, inference optimization, or production ML infrastructure.
skill.summary: Design, deploy, optimize, and monitor production machine learning systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern, four weak cryptography patterns, and one system reconnaissance pattern in SKILL.md. Review found these are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and machine learning terminology, not executable code or malicious behavior. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or hidden execution intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Resource Reference
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 26 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The line only uses Markdown backticks around resources/implementation-playbook.md and instructs the assistant to open a local reference if examples are needed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks delimit a Markdown file path, not executable syntax. No shell command, interpreter invocation, or dynamic user input is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 3 as weak cryptography. The line is part of the skill description for production machine learning systems and contains no cryptographic API or algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding frontmatter describes ML frameworks. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 152 as weak cryptography. The line describes ML system architecture design and does not reference cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is the response approach for designing ML systems. No encryption, hashing, key handling, or weak algorithm usage appears on this line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 161 as weak cryptography. The line is an example prompt about a recommendation system and has no cryptographic content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The phrase concerns model serving scale for recommendations. No evidence found of a cryptographic primitive or insecure implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 165 as weak cryptography. The line is an example prompt about model monitoring and drift detection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is ordinary MLOps terminology. No evidence found of cryptographic code, secret handling, or algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 111 as system reconnaissance. The line lists recommendation systems as an ML application area and does not inspect the host environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The word systems appears in a machine learning taxonomy. No evidence found of host enumeration, operating system probing, or environment discovery.

content.value_statement: Production ML work often fails at serving, monitoring, and lifecycle management. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical MLOps design and implementation.
actual_capabilities: Designs model serving architectures for real-time and batch inference.
Plans feature engineering, feature store, and data validation workflows.
Recommends PyTorch, TensorFlow, ONNX, and classical ML tooling.
Defines model evaluation, A/B testing, and rollout strategies.
Adds monitoring plans for drift, latency, throughput, and business metrics.
Suggests cost and performance optimizations for production ML workloads.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or packaged implementation assets.
Cannot verify live cloud quotas, costs, or account configuration.
Requires project-specific data schemas, traffic targets, and compliance needs.
Recommendations still need testing against real datasets and infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Deploy a Model Service
use_cases.description: Create a serving plan with APIs, containers, scaling, and monitoring for a trained model.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Pipeline
use_cases.description: Design batch and real-time feature workflows with validation and freshness checks.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Model Rollouts
use_cases.description: Prepare A/B tests, champion-challenger comparisons, and rollback criteria for model releases.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first production deployment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain a production ML architecture for my model. Include serving, data flow, monitoring, and retraining at a beginner level.

prompt_templates.title: Design the Serving Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a model serving layer for my use case. Cover API shape, batching, caching, scaling, observability, and failure handling.

prompt_templates.title: Create an MLOps Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an MLOps plan for training, registry, deployment, monitoring, retraining, and governance. Include practical milestones and checks.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Production Inference
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced team improving cost and latency.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this inference workload and propose optimizations for latency, throughput, cost, memory use, and rollout safety.

output_examples.input: Design a real-time recommendation service for high traffic.
output: Architecture with online features, low-latency serving, cache strategy, and fallback behavior.
Deployment plan with autoscaling, canary release steps, metrics, and rollback thresholds.
Monitoring checklist for data drift, latency, error rate, and recommendation quality.

output_examples.input: Plan model monitoring for a fraud classifier.
output: Metric groups for data quality, model quality, operational health, and business impact.
Alert rules for drift, calibration changes, false positive spikes, and service degradation.
Retraining and review workflow with owners, evidence, and approval gates.

best_practices: Provide traffic targets, latency goals, model type, and data freshness requirements.
Ask for monitoring, rollback, and retraining plans before deployment.
Validate recommendations with tests, load checks, and production-like datasets.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy only for offline accuracy without operational metrics.
Do not skip data validation for training and inference inputs.
Do not choose complex infrastructure before defining scale and reliability needs.

faq.question: Can this skill write production ML code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation and produce code suggestions, but teams must test and adapt them.

faq.question: Does it support PyTorch and TensorFlow?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers PyTorch 2.x, TensorFlow 2.x, ONNX, and common classical ML tools.

faq.question: Can it help with model monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers drift, quality metrics, latency, throughput, and business outcome monitoring.

faq.question: Is it limited to cloud deployments?
faq.answer: No. It also addresses containers, Kubernetes, batch systems, edge deployment, and local architecture planning.

faq.question: Can it choose the best model for my data?
faq.answer: It can recommend evaluation approaches, but final model choice requires experiments on your dataset.

faq.question: Does it handle compliance requirements?
faq.answer: It can include governance, lineage, and access control planning, but legal review may still be required.
